# What’s on in Cheltenham | Best events & festivals in Cheltenham all year-round

## What's on in Cheltenham - The Festival Town

**Welcome to Cheltenham – The Festival Town – where there’s always something happening.**

From world-famous horse racing and jazz to street art, science, literature and food, Cheltenham’s year-round calendar of events is packed with unforgettable experiences for all ages and interests. Whether you're planning a weekend break, a family holiday, or a last-minute getaway, you’ll find a warm welcome and plenty going on.

Looking for the best events in Cheltenham? You’re in the right place.

* * *

## **A year-round festival destination**

Cheltenham’s reputation as the UK’s Festival Town is well earned. The town plays host to some of the country’s biggest and best-loved festivals, attracting global talent and thousands of visitors every year.

Expect something special in every season:

*   **Spring** – The pinnacle event of the jump-racing calendar, the Cheltenham Festival, kicks off the festival season with thrilling racing whilst the Cheltenham Jazz Festival and Cheltenham Science Festival encourage creativity and curiosity.
*   **Summer** – Enjoy music of all genres at 2000Trees, Wychwood Festival and the Cheltenham Music Festival.
*   **Autumn** – The world-renowned Cheltenham Literature Festival brings bestselling authors, bold ideas and brilliant conversations.
*   **Winter** – The festive season sparkles with Christmas markets, light trails, a magical covered ice rink and exciting racing at Cheltenham Racecourse.

And that’s just the beginning. Our events calendar also features street food festivals, outdoor cinema, artisan markets, West End quality theatre, comedy nights, and much more. Big or small, there’s always something to discover.

text:
Crafts, Fairs & Markets in Cheltenham
Shopping events, crafts, fairs & markets 
Cheltenham is a wonderful destination to enjoy pop-up markets, offering both visitors and locals the chance to mooch among local food produce or find the perfect souvenir at craft fairs.
Most of Cheltenham’s markets are held along the tree-lined Promenade and Long Gardens, right in the heart of town, including the twice-monthly Cheltenham Farmers' Market, the monthly Cheltenham Craft Market, and numerous Christmas markets.
In The Suffolks, the road is closed to traffic seven times a year and becomes fully pedestrianised for The Suffolks Sunday Markets, featuring a host of independent traders. This much-loved event brings together a fabulous array of artisan foods, local producers, antiques, homeware, crafts, and clothing, creating a bustling, lively market street.
Cheltenham Town Hall regularly hosts touring and internationally acclaimed markets, with everything from vintage clothing and art to end-of-line sales and handmade crafts, including the Cheltenham Craft Festival. Cheltenham Racecourse also hosts large special interest fairs, such as Fresh: Art Fair, while a vintage market is held regularly throughout the year at Pittville Pump Room.
Beyond the town centre, venues such as Dunkertons Park and Elkstone Studios host seasonal markets throughout the year.
Use our listings below to discover more about Cheltenham’s markets...

Family Friendly Events Cheltenham
If you're visiting Cheltenham with your family, it's good to know you can keep them entertained. Of course, there are lots of family events happening all year, and a lot of things you can do for free with kids too, including our parks and gardens.
From small, one-off community events to regular family-friendly happenings, our list below includes an array of events. More are added all the time, so remember to check here before you book and add things to your itinerary (use the heart button on each event page). 
For families seeking history and adventure, the Cotswold Festival of Steam at the Gloucestershire Warwickshire Railway offers a journey through time, where the past comes alive and the heritage of steam locomotives is celebrated over a long bank holiday weekend filled with the magic of steam, picturesque landscapes, and the rich history of the Cotswolds.
It's also worth keeping an eye on our 'Planning Ahead' page for the What's On section, where we add dedicated pages with ideas for School Holidays, Half Term, Mother's / Father's Day and Bank Holidays too...

metadata.title: Live Music in Cheltenham
metadata.description: Your guide to gigs and Live Music in Cheltenham

openGraph.content: Your guide to gigs and Live Music in Cheltenham

text:
Live Music in Cheltenham
If you are into your live music, or are a budding musician waiting to be discovered, here are some of the venues across Cheltenham where you can enjoy a night out of gigs, open mic nights, comedy and more...
Cheltenham Town Hall is Cheltenham’s biggest live music venue. This stunning Regency building plays host to music, comedy and is a leading venue for the many festivals that take place in the town. 
Smokey Joe's in Bennington Street is a retro diner which hosts regular gigs, plus an open mic night on every first Wednesday of the month.
The Cotswold Inn, Portland Street is an old school pub. It holds an acoustic open mic on a Sunday, open mic on a Tuesday, and live music Saturday evenings.
The Bath Tavern is another traditional old school pub, located in Bath Street. It holds an open mic on Thursday, plus regular gigs and ‘jam nights’.
The Frog and Fiddle pub on the High Street is popular with students. With teapot cocktails, pool tables, live sport on 14 screens and live music it's a buzzing hangout. They have a separate room called ‘the barn’ where they put on regular gigs, and each year they host the "Frogfest" festival!
Cheltenham underground is a local promoter. They put on ‘secret house shows’ in Cheltenham - an intimate gig in a living room, amazing if you get a chance to catch one! Join their Facebook group to be get a ticket. They also offer live music each month at both The Swan and The Railway (bistro-pubs), as well as festivals at both these venues - Swanfest and a Cider Festival at The Railway.
The Old Restoration pub on the High Street is described as "a brilliant atmosphere in a proper, old pub". They hold an open mic night Thursday.
The 2 Pigs is Cheltenham's Premier Alternative Club and Live Music Venue with a rock/metal nightclub and gigs most Fridays and Saturdays.
Shoot Your Hoops is another Cheltenham based promoter mainly in the genres of rock / metal / punk music. They hold a lot of gigs at The 2 Pigs and The Frog and Fiddle.
Join The Strand Bar (and restaurant) on the High Street on the last Thursday of every month for Scallywags Comedy. The MC is the very talented Jon Wagstaffe. Jon’s claim to fame is that he’s Tewkesbury’s finest (and only) stand-up comic. They also host live music.
The Everyman Theatre put on gigs in their bar, and also have a small studio place where they put on gigs.

metadata.title: Free Events in Cheltenham
metadata.description: Our guide to the free events in Cheltenham that you can pop along and experience with family and friends.

openGraph.content: Our guide to the free events in Cheltenham that you can pop along and experience with family and friends.

text:
Free Events in Cheltenham
They say that nothing in life is free, but our guide proves this theory is incorrect! As well as being home to many fabulous green spaces you can see, feel, explore, discover and enjoy for free, there is also a regular rotation of free events in Cheltenham that you can pop along and experience with family and friends.
Summer Jam returns to The Brewery Quarter this summer, for free entertainment in Cheltenham’s one stop, shop-dine-stay-and-play destination. No Child Left Behind's Party in the Park is a fun-filled day for children and families to experience a day of free activities including live performances in music, dance and theatre.
Another top pick has to be our Heritage Open Days, which runs for ten days every September. Plan your staycation during this time to discover some very unique and special places which open their doors during this time for visitors.
Of course, we cannot mention free events in Cheltenham, without paying homage to the Cheltenham Festivals. With the free around town… programme supported by Cheltenham BID and free events in the festival villages for Cheltenham Jazz Festival, Literature Festival and Science Festival the Festivals team put an amazing effort to feature fantastic speakers, artists, activities and music, which anyone can enjoy for free.
Of course, as well as all the great events listed below (with more being added all the time), you can also spend hours for free in our amazing parks and gardens, taking in some of the Regency architecture. If you want to learn more about the history of Cheltenham we recommend you take advantage of this free self-guided tour. Also, remember to look out on our Cheltenham What's On guide for the free community events taking place year-round.

Classical music
Classical music in Cheltenham 
Our beautiful Regency town of Cheltenham is renowned for its vibrant classical music activity. The town's musical heritage is celebrated annually at the Cheltenham Music Festival, a prestigious event that attracts musicians and enthusiasts from around the globe. A highlight of the town’s musical calendar throughout the year include a residency from the English Symphony Orchestra. You can also enjoy performances from the Cheltenham Symphony Orchestra and Cheltenham Philharmonic, many taking place in the iconic architectural grandeur of venues such as Cheltenham Town Hall and Pittville Pump Room.
Regular programmes of chamber music take place across a variety of other venues, including those from the long-established Cheltenham Music Society.
To join a group a passionate volunteers, who all share a love of classical music in Cheltenham, we encourage you visit this website.

A highlight of the town’s musical calendar throughout the year include a residency from the English Symphony Orchestra.  You can also enjoy performances from the Cheltenham Symphony Orchestra and Cheltenham Philharmonic, many taking place in the iconic architectural grandeur of venues such as [Cheltenham Town Hall](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/listing/cheltenham-town-hall/15304301/) and [Pittville Pump Room](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/listing/pittville-pump-room/13301301/).

metadata.title: Theatre and The Arts
metadata.description: Your guide to theatre performances and the art scene in and around Cheltenham

openGraph.content: Your guide to theatre performances and the art scene in and around Cheltenham

text:
Theatre and The Arts
Cinema, theatre & the arts 
Cheltenham is blessed with a whole host of theatres, cinemas and venues that provide a packed calendar of performances all year round. Many also support our enviable Festival programme, and in fact during our Festival weeks, we get a whole host of pop-up venues too. From classics to comedy, musicals to mystery, play to pantomime, Cheltenham is blessed with a unique programme; not only that but many of the theatres also offer architectural wonders too.
Some of the main theatres and entertainment venues include:
Home to a theatre designed by renowned architect Frank Matcham, and another that was once a Regency Spa, Cheltenham boasts a variety of fantastic venues, from the historic to the purpose built. With such diversity across the town, Cheltenham is spoilt for the year-round schedule of first-class theatre performances on offer.
The Everyman Theatre, a Grade II listed building designed by Frank Matcham opened in October 1891 with a performance by Lilly Langtry in 'Lady Clancarty', and is described as an "architectural masterpiece". It is believed to be the oldest surviving complete Frank Matcham theatre, originally opened as the New Theatre and renamed as The Everyman Theatre in 1960. Cheltenham Town Hall is host to a year-round hive of activity and entertainment, offering not just theatre, but functions, festivals, exhibitions and performing arts. It was built at the turn of the twentieth century to accommodate many of the town's balls and social events - it was, quite literally built for celebrations! It incorporates the Regency Cafe and Imperial Garden Bar. Now, the two venues - which are worth a visit in their own right - host West End productions and famous names and faces, alongside musicals, traditional Christmas panto, comedy, live bands, famous faces and children's productions.
The Bacon Theatre is set in the grounds of Dean Close School and, completed in 1991, is Cheltenham's most contemporary theatre. Dean Close is also home to an original outdoor Amphitheatre which hosts a small number of plays and the Open Air Theatre Festival each year, and the Prince Michael's Hall which is an intimate concert venue. With free on-site parking and reasonable ticket prices, it offers a quality yet affordable experience.
The Screening Rooms are located within The Brewery Quarter and offer a contemporary alternative to a traditional cinema. With a meet and greet from the Concierge it's somewhere a little bit special, and also a great place to meet friends for drinks too. You can even get your meal brought to your seat! They are located within the same complex as Cineworld, an 11-screen state of the art cinema showing the latest movie releases, and a brand new IMAX screen to give that all round movie experience. With plenty of nearby parking and a variety of restaurants and bars within easy reach, it's a great place for an evening out with friends or the family.
Whether it’s an evening performance with friends or a loved one, or a matinee performance with the family or as part of a group visit to Cheltenham, not a day goes by when there isn’t a theatre performance going on somewhere!
During school holidays there are always plenty of children’s theatre performances such as Dear Zoo, Sing-a-Long Beauty and The Beast and David Walliams’ Gangsta Granny! Of course, every year The Everyman Theatre is host to the annual Christmas Pantomime. 
Putting on a performance? Remember you can add your event for free via our Event Submission Form.

# Submit an event to the Visit Cheltenham What's On Guide

## **Have you organised an event taking place in Cheltenham?**

You can promote your event for **FREE** on the [Cheltenham What's On](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/whats-on) guide by submitting event details and uploading an image below.

In 2025 the Visit Cheltenham website attracted over 2.1 million views.  You can benefit from this exposure by following the guidelines below.

## **Enhanced promotion of events**

To feature in the enhanced sections on the Visit Cheltenham website, promote events via the Visit Cheltenham social media channels, promote via lamp post banners around town or feature in our [fortnightly newsletter](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/your-visit/enewsletter-sign-up) to 32K+ subscribers please see our [rate pack](https://marketingcheltenham.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/2025-digital-rate-card-min.pdf). If you have any questions please contact [\[email protected\]](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#721f13001917061b1c15321f13001917061b1c15111a171e06171c1a131f5c111d5c071954510a334249) 

## **Guidelines for listing an event for free**

\- The Visit Cheltenham team will only approve local events that they feel will be of interest to local people and visitors to Cheltenham. 

\- Once your event has been submitted it will be reviewed within three working days. 

**\-** Events will only be approved with a good quality, **horizontal orientation** image.

\- Images need to be **900 x 606 pixels.** Please be aware that there is a file size limit of 1MB. 

\- Copyright: You must ensure that you have permission to use the image on a third party website. Any uploaded images may be shared in various places on the Visit Cheltenham website and social media channels.

\- Posters and vertical orientation images are **not permitted**, as the format of our website does not show them correctly.

\- Please do not use full capital letters in titles, or in the event description. 

\- If you have not included key information, or have uploaded an image that does not fit the sizing requirements specified, your event will be rejected. 

Please enter your product information below.

## **Contact details**

If you have any questions about how to upload your event, or how to change an existing event listing please contact - [\[email protected\]](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#600516050e1413200401140114080913140c054e030f0d)

Cheltenham Jazz Festival – Showcasing global stars, emerging talent and unforgettable performances each spring.
Cheltenham Music Festival – A summer celebration of classical music, blending tradition with contemporary creativity.

These iconic events bring a unique energy to the town, with buzzing streets, lively hospitality and a truly memorable atmosphere.

You’ll also find street food markets, outdoor cinema, comedy nights and live performances happening throughout the year.

Spring – Racing fever takes over with the Cheltenham Festival, alongside the Jazz and Science Festivals.
Summer – Music fills the air with festivals, outdoor events and the legendary Cheltenham Cricket Festival.

markdown:
# Cheltenham 2026 festivals & major events guide

Cheltenham’s reputation as [The Festival Town](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/explore-the-area/festival-town-link/) is at the heart of everything we do. Throughout the year, our Regency town comes alive with world-class festivals, major sporting events and cultural celebrations that attract visitors from across the globe.

From the roar of the racecourse to inspiring talks, live music and family-friendly experiences, Cheltenham’s events calendar is packed with unforgettable moments in every season.

## **World-famous festivals**

Cheltenham is home to an internationally acclaimed programme of festivals, bringing together leading voices in literature, science, music and more.

The Cheltenham Festivals series

The town’s flagship cultural programme includes four major festivals:

*   [Cheltenham Literature Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-literature-festival/254484301/) – The world’s oldest literature festival, welcoming bestselling authors, thinkers and cultural icons each autumn.
*   [Cheltenham Science Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-science-festival/250891301/) – A celebration of discovery and innovation, making science exciting and accessible for all ages.
*   [Cheltenham Jazz Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-jazz-festival/149463301/) – Showcasing global stars, emerging talent and unforgettable performances each spring.
*   [Cheltenham Music Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-music-festival/15994301/) – A summer celebration of classical music, blending tradition with contemporary creativity.

Together, these festivals transform Cheltenham into a hub of ideas, creativity and culture throughout the year.

## **Major sporting events**

Cheltenham is just as famous for its sporting calendar, with high profile events that draw passionate crowds and global attention.

*   [Cheltenham Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/the-cheltenham-festival/274525301/) – The pinnacle of the National Hunt racing calendar, held every March at Cheltenham Racecourse, the Home of Jump Racing. Expect four thrilling days of world-class racing, including the legendary Gold Cup.
*   [Cheltenham Cricket Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-cricket-festival/255455301/) – The world’s longest-running cricket festival, offering a quintessentially English summer experience in the spectacular grounds of Cheltenham College.

These iconic events bring a unique energy to the town, with buzzing streets, lively hospitality and a truly memorable atmosphere.

## **Music, food & contemporary culture**

Beyond the headline festivals, Cheltenham’s calendar is packed with exciting events celebrating music, food and modern culture.

*   [2000trees Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/2000trees-festival/282690301/) – An award-winning independent music festival just outside Cheltenham, known for its diverse line-up and friendly vibe.
*   [Wychwood Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/wychwood-festival/270123301/) – Winner of the Family Festival of the Year, is a much-loved family-friendly festival combining music, comedy, workshops and great food with a spectacular setting at the foot of the Cotswold Hills.  
*   [Cheltenham Food & Drink Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-food-%26-drink-festival/50752301/) – A celebration of local & UK best produce, street food and culinary talent, perfect for food lovers.

You’ll also find street food markets, outdoor cinema, comedy nights and live performances happening throughout the year.

## **When are the best times of year to visit Cheltenham?**

No matter when you visit, there’s always something to look forward to in Cheltenham:

*   Spring – Racing fever takes over with the Cheltenham Festival, alongside the Jazz and Science Festivals.
*   Summer – Music fills the air with festivals, outdoor events and the legendary Cheltenham Cricket Festival.
*   Autumn – The Literature Festival brings big names and bold ideas to our Regency town.
*   Winter – Festive markets, seasonal events and racing create a magical end to the year.

## **Plan your visit to Cheltenham around attending festivals and events**

Cheltenham’s festivals and major events are some of the best in the UK – and they’re the perfect reason to plan your trip.

*   Browse our what’s on in Cheltenham listings to see upcoming events
*   Book your stay early for popular festivals
*   Explore our itineraries to make the most of your visit

Whether you’re here for a world-famous festival, a major sporting event or a spontaneous day out, you’ll find something extraordinary happening in Cheltenham all year round.

text:
Autumn in Cheltenham | Best autumn events in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire
With its parks and gardens turning brilliant shades of red, orange and gold and the elegant Regency architecture taking on a warm and inviting charm, Cheltenham truly comes alive for the autumn. 
The season kicks off with the return of Cheltenham Heritage Open Days, a 10-day impressive programme featuring 55 open buildings, plays, walks and talks. Followed by Cheltenham Literature Festival, packed with 400+ events and 500 speakers delivering inspiring talks, big debates, live music. As the world’s longest-running literature festival, the annual event leads the way in celebrating the written and spoken word, presenting the best new voices in fiction and poetry alongside literary greats and high-profile speakers.
Horse racing fans will flock to the home of jump racing, Cheltenham Racecourse, for two days in October to attend The Showcase and again for The November Meeting, an annual unmissable three-day meeting. Meanwhile train fans will head to the GWSR Autumn Showcase to enjoy an intensive timetable with both steam and heritage diesels in action and workshops open to see.
As mornings grow crisp and frosty, Cheltenham's autumn also heralds the approaching festive season. November brings lots of festivities, including the return of the magical undercover Cheltenham Ice Rink.
Autumn is the perfect season to enjoy delicious food and luckily in Cheltenham we are blessed with many fabulous places to eat and drink. So treat yourself to a Sunday roast, wrap up warm and keep scrolling for the best events this autumn in Cheltenham.

The festive season is just over the horizon, and Cheltenham gears up for a magical Christmas period this month with several official Christmas launch and light switch on events. 

The thundering of hooves returns to Cheltenham Racecourse this month as well at the [November Meeting](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/whats-on/the-november-meeting-p2745403), with Family Fun Day plus live music, performances, craft fairs and much more! Theatre lovers can enjoy Robin Hood and His Merry Men, a pantomime at The Everyman Theatre, perfect for the whole family.

text:
Active & Sports Events in Cheltenham
Believe it or not, our humble town has quite the sporting prowess and active history!
Of course, we cannot think 'active' without mentioning one of Cheltenham's most famous sons, Edward Wilson, Explorer. Scientist, natural historian, polar explorer and ornithologist (who also dabbled in painting), was renowned for his love of the countryside and being active, ultimately became a naturalist. He ultimately went on to become a polar explorer with Scott, taking part in the Discovery Expedition to Antarctica in 1902, an expedition which became at the time the most southerly trek ever by an explorer. He then, of course, took part in the ill-fated Terra Nova expedition as Scott's Chief of the Scientific Staff. Despite making great exploratory strides, reaching the South Pole, they were pipped to the post by Amundson and his team just five weeks earlier. 
Aside from famous sons, the Cheltenham Cricket Festival is hosted every year at Cheltenham College, we have one of the few remaining outdoor lido's in the country, we're home to horseracing and there's plenty of outdoor pursuits including clay pigeon shooting for example. We also host a number of county tournaments and events, so keep your eyes peeled for those!
For running enthusiasts, there are plenty of events to choose from, including Run Cheltenham and the Cheltenham Running Festival at the Cheltenham Racecourse.

Believe it or not, our humble town has quite the sporting prowess and active history!

Of course, we cannot think 'active' without mentioning one of Cheltenham's most famous sons, Edward Wilson, Explorer. Scientist, natural historian, polar explorer and ornithologist (who also dabbled in painting), was renowned for his love of the countryside and being active, ultimately became a naturalist. He ultimately went on to become a polar explorer with Scott, taking part in the Discovery Expedition to Antarctica in 1902, an expedition which became at the time the most southerly trek ever by an explorer. He then, of course, took part in the ill-fated Terra Nova expedition as Scott's Chief of the Scientific Staff. Despite making great exploratory strides, reaching the South Pole, they were pipped to the post by Amundson and his team just five weeks earlier. 

Aside from famous sons, the [Cheltenham Cricket Festival](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/event/cheltenham-cricket-festival/255455301/) is hosted every year at Cheltenham College, we have one of the few remaining [outdoor lido's](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/explore-the-area/regency-cheltenham/) in the country, we're home to horseracing and there's plenty of outdoor pursuits including [clay pigeon shooting](https://www.visitcheltenham.com/listing/ian-coley-sporting-ltd/344460301/) for example. metadata.title: Cheltenham Christmas Gift Market -
metadata.description: Experience festive shopping at the Cheltenham Christmas Gift Market, hosted by one of the leading market operators - Cotswold Markets, In 2025, Cotswold Markets are bringing TWO Christmas markets to Cheltenham’s Promenade and Long Gardens, in front of the Municipal Offices. The first will be a Christmas Gift Market, taking place on the 20 - 23 November, 10am - 6pm. This will be a fantastic selection of retail gifts such as homewares and food & drink, plus hot food and mulled wine to enjoy whilst you browse. The Market will have 40 Christmas chalets for the four-day period. The second will be our traditional Arts & Crafts Christmas Market, taking place on the Promenade from the 11 to the 23 December. Local traders and creators will demonstrate their fantastic home made crafts. Pottery, original art, glassware, jewellery and much more will be on offer. Location of the Cheltenham Christmas Gift Market... Market stalls will run along the pedestrianised Promenade and Long Gardens in front of the Municipal Office, providing shoppers with lots of opportunities to pick up unique gifts. Entrance is free. Other Christmas events in Cheltenham... Christmas Markets are just one of many magical yuletide events visitors will be able to experience in Cheltenham this year. Check out our guide for more inspiration -  Christmas events in Cheltenham. Be one of the first to hear about the best events, latest news and special offers in Cheltenham... metadata.title: Santa Express with GWSR -
metadata.description: Santa Express – Gloucestershire Warwickshire Steam Railway - Tickets now on sale. Climb aboard the GWSR’s Santa Express for a festive train ride through the Cotswolds. Your journey begins at Cheltenham Racecourse Station (or at Toddington on 13th December only). Remember to visit the steam locomotive before you leave the station. Each child will receive a goody bag when you arrive at your departure station containing: A Santa fun book with puzzles and activities A small pen and pack of pencils An “I Have Seen Santa” badge A small pack of sweets Visiting Santa & refreshments To help manage the visit smoothly: Coaches A, B & C – There will be an opportunity to visit Santa first and receive a gift, then collect your free refreshments from the café and enjoy the other activities. Coaches E, F & G – Start with your free refreshments from the café, then enjoy the activities before visiting Santa, and receive a gift. What’s included Free refreshments for all: mince pies and mulled wine for adults, chocolate and a drink for children Singing Penguins on Platform 1 Photo opportunities: in the snowy sleigh, with selfie frames, or meeting the Snowman or Reindeer (elves will take photos on your phone/camera) Write and post your letter to Santa (blank letter in your booklet) And of course your all-important present from Santa. ‍Santa Express dates 2025 November: 22, 23, 29, 30 December: 6, 7, 13, 14, 20, 21, 22, 23

metadata.title: Cheltenham Literature Festival -
metadata.description: The globally renowned Times and The Sunday Times Cheltenham Literature Festival will return to The Festival Town, 9 October – 18 October 2026. The impressive ten-day programme in 2025 featured 400+ events and 500 speakers. Visitors in 2026 can expect inspiring talks, big debates, live music and unforgettable moments in the Festival Village and at venues throughout our Regency town. Our top tip is to make the most of it - Explore the FREE-to-enter Festival Village, browse the bookshops, catch surprise performances, or simply relax with food and a drink in hand. Watch this space for the full line up coming soon.  What is the Cheltenham Literature Festival? As the world’s longest-running literature festival, the annual event leads the way in celebrating the written and spoken word, presenting the best new voices in fiction and poetry alongside literary greats and high-profile speakers. It brings together some of the biggest names in publishing, award-winning authors of fiction and non-fiction join poets, spoken word artists and new voices to delve into a range of topics including travel and adventure, food and drink, lifestyle, history, art and religion. Past guests have included David Cameron, Hillary Clinton, Brian Cox and David Attenborough.  The schools and family programme features events to inspire and entertain. Past events have included draw-alongs, storytelling, facts, fiction and the prestigious CLiPPA (Centre for Literacy in Primary Poetry Award) award for published poetry for children. Visit the Cheltenham Festivals website for full details and to book tickets.  For more unmissable events in Cheltenham check out our helpful guides - Cheltenham festivals & what's on. Hotels get booked up quickly for the Cheltenham Literature Festival, we recommend you book in advance to avoid disappointment.  To plan your stay in visit our Cheltenham hotels, Cheltenham restaurants, Cheltenham shops & Cheltenham weekend breaks.

metadata.title: The November Meeting -
metadata.description: The November Meeting, Cheltenham Racecourse, Friday 13 to Sunday 15 November 2026 Racing’s ultimate weekender is here –  a meeting where the atmosphere is electric and Festival-bound talent takes centre stage.  The weekend starts with Countryside Day, celebrating rural life and tradition alongside top-class sport, before diving into Super Saturday, where the energy is electric, racing reaches its peak against the stunning Cotswold hills, and the post-racing celebrations raise the roof. Finally, enjoy the community spirit of Carnival Sunday, where there's something for everyone to enjoy.   Three days, three distinct experiences — why not take advantage of our multi-day ticket offer and enjoy each day’s unique atmosphere for yourself?  Please note: For this fixture, Club/Tatts will be sold as Club on Friday and Sunday, and no badge will be required. This means you can purchase a multi-day Club ticket for Saturday and receive a Friday or Sunday ticket at 20% off.   What can you expect from The November Meeting at Cheltenham Racecourse? Day One - Countryside Day Friday is known as countryside day, celebrating the very best of rural life and top-class sport — a day for anyone who loves the countryside and the traditions that make it special. Arrive early to enjoy the pre-racing Parade Ring displays, including a look at the different types of hounds used by local packs. Following this, join raceday presenters for  The Preview Show,  live from the Winner’s Enclosure. They'll be joined by racing greats and special guests to preview the afternoon’s action, share exclusive insights, and hopefully tip some winners!  Day Two - Super Saturday, featuring the Paddy Power Gold Cup Steeplechase One of the most anticipated race days outside of the Cheltenham Festival. Where the energy is palpable and the racing at its finest, leading owners, trainers, and jockeys compete for season-defining victories – all set against the stunning Cotswold hills. Pre-racing join raceday presenters for  The Preview Show,  live from The Winner’s Enclosure. They'll be joined by racing greats and special guests to preview the afternoon’s action, share exclusive insights, and hopefully tip some winners!  Day Three - Carnival Sunday  Wrap up The November Meeting in style with Carnival Sunday, a community celebration of racing, music, and family fun at Cheltenham. Six thrilling races, plus the Shetland Pony Race (12:10pm) and the hilarious Sue Ryder Mascot Race (1:25pm) – it’s non-stop action from start to finish! Enjoy live music all day in The Guinness Village & Tattersalls Sales Arena and join in The CADBURY Family Fun Zone in The Centaur – Chocolate, circus shows, arts & crafts, face painting, inflatables, and meet the Minions & Freddo! Relax with friends, soak up the atmosphere, and celebrate all things Cheltenham. Please note - Family Fun entertainment is only available in the Club/Tatts enclosure.
